title: Shopify Theme Change SEO Checklist: Protect Your Rankings
body_markdown:
Why Shopify stores lose rankings after a theme change

Swapping your Shopify theme feels cosmetic. To Google it often is not. URL structures shift, heading hierarchies flatten, structured data disappears, and internal links that took years to accumulate quietly break. Most stores see a 10-30% organic dip in the eight weeks after a redesign, and most of it is preventable.

The three things that actually break

1. Collection and product URL handles

Themes do not change URLs by themselves, but redesigns usually ship with a catalog cleanup, and that does. Every renamed handle without a 301 redirect is a dead backlink and a lost ranking. Export your top 100 organic landing pages from Search Console before the migration and diff them after.

2. Heading structure

Many modern themes wrap product titles in div tags styled to look like headings. Crawlers do not read CSS. If your new theme demotes the only h1 on a product page to a styled span, you have removed the strongest on-page signal that page had.

3. Structured data

Product schema, review stars, price and availability markup are theme-rendered on most stores. A new theme can silently drop them, and rich results vanish from the SERP within days. Validate your top templates with the Rich Results test the same hour you publish the theme.

The 48-hour post-launch checklist

Crawl the store with the new theme live. Compare h1 coverage, canonical tags, schema presence and internal link counts against the pre-launch crawl. Re-submit the sitemap, watch Search Console coverage for spikes in 404s, and fix redirects the same day you find them. Rankings recover fastest when the gap between break and fix is measured in hours, not weeks.

Ryze runs this comparison automatically after every theme deploy, flags the diffs that matter for rankings, and ships the redirects for you.

meta_title: Shopify Theme Change SEO Checklist: Protect Your Rankings
